persons.arguments_against: Two stints are involved; the 'Adolphe' rendering is a single printed initial 'A.'. But the entry is Sir J. P. R. A. Caron (Adolphe-Philippe Caron), Canadian Minister of Militia and Defence, K.C.M.G. (1885) — both stints carry that exact office, the K.C.M.G., and Canada across overlapping years. They are one person under two name renderings; a distinct same-surname Canadian militia minister with the K.C.M.G. is not credible.
persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person serving the same colony in the same years and offices, or a printed birth/death date excluding these years.

notes: production high-value slice, hand-adjudicated (Claude Code, in-session)
